items.headline: Global Markets Face 'Risk-Off' Sentiment Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Inflation Worries
items.summary: Global stock markets are experiencing a 'Risk-Off' sentiment today, largely stemming from the Asia-Pacific region and impacting U.S. technology benchmarks. Geopolitical tensions, particularly surrounding the Iran conflict and the reported closure of the Strait of Hormuz, have caused oil prices to surge, raising renewed inflation concerns. U.S. stock futures pointed lower this morning, with E mini S&P 500 contracts down about 1.5%, Nasdaq 100 futures off nearly 2%, and small-cap Russell 2000 futures lower by approximately 2.6%. The U.S. 10-year Treasury yield remains elevated near 4.04%, contributing to higher borrowing costs for businesses and households. Despite a mixed close on Monday, where the S&P 500 posted a small gain, the Dow dipped, and the Nasdaq rose, today's market opened under pressure. Economic data releases, including US ISM Manufacturing and Services PMIs, Unemployment Claims, Non-Farm Payrolls, and Retail Sales, are anticipated later in the week, which could further influence market direction. Not financial advice.

items.statContext: Ended up 0.04% on March 3. Futures down 1.5% in pre-market.
Rose 0.36% on March 3. Futures off nearly 2% in pre-market.
Dipped 0.15% on March 3. Futures fell 1.6% in pre-market.

items.headline: Plug Power Surges on Strong Q4 Earnings Beat
items.summary: Shares of Plug Power ($PLUG) climbed 10% after reporting a Q4 earnings beat, highlighting approximately 13% year-over-year revenue growth and a significant gross margin improvement to +2.4% from -122.5% in Q4 2024. The company also reported a record $187 million in electrolyzer revenue for 2025.

items.headline: Venture Global Jumps on Strong Earnings and New LNG Deal
items.summary: Venture Global ($VG) shares surged 17.44% following strong Q4 and full-year earnings, coupled with news of a new long-term LNG deal.

items.headline: Coherent Climbs as Barclays Raises Price Target
items.summary: Coherent ($COHR) stock gained 15.44% after Barclays raised its price target and NVIDIA participated in a large private placement.

items.headline: MongoDB Plunges on Mixed Guidance
items.summary: MongoDB ($MDB) shares plunged 24% after the company issued mixed guidance, with investors focusing on softer near-term expectations despite otherwise solid results.

items.headline: Estée Lauder Companies Falls
items.summary: Estée Lauder Companies ($EL) shares fell 8.48% today.

items.headline: Elevance Health Slips After Executive Transition
items.summary: Elevance Health ($ELV) slipped 8.10% following an executive leadership transition and an advisor role update.

items.headline: Iran Conflict Escalates, Fueling Oil Price Surge and Economic Uncertainty
items.summary: The ongoing U.S.-Israeli attacks against Iran and Iran's reported closure of the Strait of Hormuz are significantly impacting global markets. Oil prices surged due to threats to world energy supplies, with benchmark U.S. crude rising to $74.47 a barrel and Brent crude to $81.30 a barrel on Monday. The International Monetary Fund (IMF) stated that these developments make the global economic outlook more uncertain. While the U.S. economy is more buffered from energy shocks, the global impact on trade, prices, and investment could undermine growth. Inflation worries are renewed, particularly in regions like Tokyo, where rising oil prices fueled a further stock plunge today.

items.headline: Fed Expected to Hold Rates Steady Amid Market Volatility
items.summary: The CME Group's FedWatch tool indicates a 97.3% likelihood of the Federal Reserve leaving current interest rates unchanged in March. Historically, the S&P 500 has achieved a median one-year return of 10% following interest rate cuts, with an 11% return when cuts made during recessions are excluded.

items.headline: Key Companies Report Earnings; Broadcom Anticipated Tomorrow
items.summary: Several companies reported earnings today, March 3, 2026. AutoZone ($AZO) and Best Buy ($BBY) both beat Q4 earnings estimates. Thor Industries ($THO), Sea Limited ($SE), and Marex Group ($MRX) were also among those reporting. On ($ONON) announced annual net sales exceeding CHF 3.0 billion for the first time and anticipates at least 23% year-over-year growth in 2026. Broadcom ($AVGO) is slated to deliver its Q1 2026 earnings after market close on Wednesday, March 4, with semiconductors and infrastructure software in focus.

items.headline: Energy and Materials Lead as 'Underdogs' Emerge in Early 2026
items.summary: In early 2026, 'underdog' sectors such as Energy, Materials, and Industrials are leading the way, a trend that often foreshadows increases in economic activity and earnings. These sectors, along with technology and healthcare, are expected to be top American industries for the year, driven by structural tailwinds including federal policy support and technological advancement. The communication services and information technology sectors have demonstrated strong performance over the last three and ten years, respectively, largely fueled by advancements in artificial intelligence. Conversely, in the ASX 200, Industrials, Communications, Utilities, and Financials were lagging today.
